methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsc.edu UCSC generative AI policy academic integrity artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official UCSC TLC, ITS, campus AI, and news sources related to generative AI.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsc.edu UCSC AI tools approved privacy data ChatGPT Gemini Copilot
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official campus AI FAQ for tool availability and data-handling guidance.
The ITS safe-use guide linked to the official comparison page for campus-licensed AI tools.
The TLC resource library provided official teaching guidance on academic integrity and course AI-use policies.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found current official UCSC AI evidence on the campus AI FAQ, ITS AI safety guide, ITS licensed-tool comparison page, and TLC teaching resources. A 2024 official news security statement was recorded as limited because newer ITS and AI Council pages provide the current data-protection and tool-availability guidance.

artifacts.claimText: UC Santa Cruz's campus AI FAQ states that student AI use in coursework is governed at the individual course or assignment level, rather than by a single campus-wide student AI-use rule in that FAQ.

citation.citationTitle: FAQ - Generative Artificial Intelligence at UC Santa Cruz
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: FAQ - Generative Artificial Intelligence at UC Santa Cruz
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At UC Santa Cruz, instructors set policy on use of AI at the individual course level. In some cases, a policy might be set at the level of an assignment.

artifacts.evidenceLocator: Teaching and Learning section, question: As a student, can I use AI in my coursework?

artifacts.claimText: UCSC ITS says users should never enter P4 data into any AI tool, and that P3 or P4 data is prohibited in unlicensed AI tools.

citation.publisher: UC Santa Cruz Information Technology Services
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: University standards prohibit entering sensitive data — P3 or P4 data, including personally identifiable and FERPA-protected records — into any unlicensed tool.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: ITS says P3/P4 data is prohibited in unlicensed AI tools and P4 data should never be entered in AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Best practices for using AI tools, Protect UCSC data

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim scoped to the campus AI FAQ; it does not claim a binding university-wide policy.
Approved as a current ITS tool-availability claim; access is limited to the roles stated in the source.
Approved because the ITS guide uses explicit prohibition language for P3/P4 data in unlicensed tools and P4 data in AI tools generally.
Approved as teaching guidance only; not elevated to a binding campus policy.
Approved as instructor support guidance from TLC/AIO; wording avoids making a mandatory policy claim.
Approved as a narrow research guidance claim tied to the FAQ's human-subjects research language.

artifacts.title: University of California, Santa Cruz AI Policy Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Official UCSC sources show current campus-licensed AI tool availability for staff and contractors, data-protection boundaries for licensed and unlicensed AI tools, course-level student AI-use governance, TLC/AIO academic-integrity response guidance, and IRB contact guidance for human-subjects research involving AI. No central binding campus-wide student AI-use policy was found in the checked official sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not marked canonical.
The 2024 news statement was recorded as limited because newer official ITS and AI Council pages supersede its current tool-availability language.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
